qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Publish-service reader can read arbitrary workspace files through getFile
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is a publish-reader authorization and file-scope bug in POST /api/file/getFile, while the advisory is a double-URL-decoding path traversal in /export/ handled by serveExport(). They reach different endpoints, different code, and different root causes.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's serveExport() double-decode path traversal root cause. The only qualifying finding is a separate publish-service reader file-read issue in /api/file/getFile.
evidence: The advisory describes kernel/server/serve.go serveExport() trimming /export/, then calling url.PathUnescape() on the already-decoded path before filepath.Join(exportBaseDir, decodedPath), enabling double-encoded ../ traversal.

The upstream fix for GHSA-hjh7-r5w8-5872 adds gulu.File.IsSubPath(exportBaseDir, fullPath) inside serveExport() and also extends sensitive-path blocking for .db and .log files, confirming the vulnerable code path is the /export/ handler.

The accepted qualifying finding instead routes through publish service authentication, RoleReader acceptance, POST /api/file/getFile, and CheckAbsPathAccessableByPublishAccess(), which is a different endpoint family and a different missing-authorization boundary.
